description_long: This sculpture depicts a classical male figure standing in a relaxed contrapposto pose, with one arm draped over a support and the other hand holding a piece of drapery. The figure is adorned with a laurel wreath on his head and a pelt or animal skin draped over his left shoulder. The anatomical details are finely rendered, with visible musculature and naturalistic proportions. At the figure's feet, there is a tree trunk or support structure, and a cylindrical object, possibly a pedestal or a symbolic element. The surface is smooth and appears to be carved from a single block of white marble or stone, with subtle textural variations. The overall style is reminiscent of Greco-Roman statuary, emphasizing idealized human form and classical themes.
description: A finely detailed classical male figure stands with a laurel wreath and draped animal skin, supported by a tree trunk and cylindrical object, carved in smooth white stone. The sculpture evokes Greco-Roman artistry and idealized anatomy.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full frontal anatomy, laurel wreath, draped animal skin, and both supporting elements at the feet.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the face, the draped skin, and the cylindrical object, with the arm and hand positions more visible.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back drapery, the figure's posture, and the detailed support structure, with the laurel wreath and animal skin visible from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizes the figure's profile, the draped skin, and the tree trunk support, with the hand and arm positions distinct.
attribute_subject_matter: classical male human figure with laurel wreath and animal skin
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture is isolated against a plain background.
